ogTitle: Bonny Hills vs Bonnyrigg: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bonny Hills and Bonnyrigg.
heading: Bonny Hills vs Bonnyrigg — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bonny Hills, New South Wales and Bonnyrigg, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bonnyrigg has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Bonny Hills (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bonny Hills or Bonnyrigg safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bonny Hills has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Bonnyrigg scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bonny Hills scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Bonnyrigg scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bonny Hills or Bonnyrigg?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bonnyrigg has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
